tier: A2-3.2 increment 3: in-place COW delete, rename, and write (create-or-replace) round-trip directories and their children
host: Local WSL2 archlinux (qemu-system-x86_64 -enable-kvm, macOS Sequoia recovery, apfs_kext 2332.101.1) with a 64 MiB dir+child image passed through
change: Extended the full-tree round-trip from the insert commit (increment 2) to the delete, rename, and write (create-or-replace) commits. buildDeleteFileList and buildRenameFileList no longer hardcode the root parent and match the target among root files only (a directory child of the same name is untouched); commitInPlaceFileDelete takes an ApfsDeleteRequest carrying directories; ApfsRenameRequest and ApfsRootFileWriteRequest carry directories; all thread through buildFsTreeNodes. The image and raw delete/rename/write entry points use collectFullFsTree. Every in-place COW file mutation now preserves directories, so the temporary directory fail-closed guard (and the now-unused collectAllRootFiles / collectExistingRootFiles) were removed. Flat-root containers are byte-identical to the certified single-level layout.
scenarios: From a container with directory 'docs'/child 'a.txt' (data) plus root files r1.txt and r2.txt: commit-image-file-delete r1.txt, commit-image-file-rename r2.txt->r2x.txt, and commit-image-file-write (replace) r2.txt - each preserves the directory and its child.
linux_apfsck: apfsck -cw clean (only the pre-existing benign 'Data stream xfield: wrong flags.' note) on the delete, rename, and write/replace results

apple_fsck_apfs.certified_path: the write/replace path (commitImageOnlyFileWrite = delete + insert, the production File Management write primitive)
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_mount_read: the macOS kernel auto-mounted DCTEST read-write; cat /Volumes/DCTEST/docs/a.txt returned 'child-file-content' (the directory child preserved through the replace) and cat /Volumes/DCTEST/r2.txt returned 'r2-REPLACED-longer' (the replaced content)
apple_fsck_apfs.kernel_continuation: writer emitted xid 6 (insert into dir+child x2 then delete + insert for the replace); the kernel mounted read-write and continued the checkpoint to transaction ID 8
apple_fsck_apfs.command: fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk1 (unmounted after the kernel auto-mounted it read-write)
apple_fsck_apfs.container_verdict: The container /dev/disk1 appears to be OK.
apple_fsck_apfs.volume_verdict: The volume /dev/rdisk1s1 appears to be OK.
phases_clean: extent ref tree (preserved child extent + replaced file extent)
apple_fsck_apfs.rename_note: rename does not change any directory's child count, so its valence is identical to the source; apfsck-clean plus the shared buildFsTreeRecords/buildFsTreeNodes path the write cert exercises cover it.

note: With insert/delete/rename/write all round-tripping directories, the remaining A2-3.2 work is the four directory mutations themselves on the COW engine (create-directory, delete-directory, directory-child write, directory-child delete) plus wiring the File Management bridge's directory routes off the legacy raw writer.
